etymology.description: 肉(肉) (ròu, "meat") + 犬(犬) (quǎn, "dog") = meat associated with dogs
etymology.interpretation: The character 肰(rán) combines the meanings of "meat" and "dog," suggesting a situation or context where meat is related to dogs. This component is now mainly used in combination with the fire radical to form 然 (rán, "thus") in modern Chinese.
